zh.Salm, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, France: 法國阿爾薩斯巴-爾薩爾姆
zh.Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lorraine, France: 法國洛萊姆斯巴勒迪克
zh.lifeSummary: Marguerite de Bar le Duc是Thiébaut Ier de Bar和Ermesende de Luxembourg I的女兒，出生在法國洛萊姆斯巴勒迪克。她於1221年與Henri de Dampierre von Salm III結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1259年之後，埋葬在比利時盧布格阿爾隆。

zh-hans.Salm, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, France: 法国阿爾薩斯巴-爾薩爾姆
zh-hans.Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lorraine, France: 法国洛萊姆斯巴勒迪克
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Marguerite de Bar le Duc是Thiébaut Ier de Bar和Ermesende de Luxembourg I的女儿，出生在法国洛萊姆斯巴勒迪克。她于1221年与Henri de Dampierre von Salm III结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1259年之后，埋葬在比利时盧布格阿爾隆。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bar
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Moshe, Giora, Ilan, Arie, Eliahu, Hadassa, Haim, Menashe, Mordechai, Nadav, Nir, Noam.  French Dany, Francois, Henri.  German Hans, Gerhard, Willi.
Jewish (Ashkenazic; also Bär): from the Yiddish male personal name Ber (see Baer 2).
German (Bär): variant of Baer 1 ‘bear’ and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this and of its cognate Bähr (see Bahr 2).

variants.name: Baer
meanings: German (also Bär): from Middle High German ber ‘bear’, a nickname for someone thought to resemble the animal in some way, a metonymic occupational name for someone who kept a performing bear, or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a bear. In some cases it may derive from a personal name containing this element. Compare Bar 2.
Jewish (Ashkenazic; also Bär): from the Yiddish male personal name Ber, from Yiddish ber ‘bear’. Compare Bar 1.
Germanized form (also Bär) of Sorbian Běr: see Bar 3.

variants.name: Dubar
meanings: French (mainly Nord): habitational name, with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’, denoting someone from any of several places called Bar (see Bar ).

variants.name: Debar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Laurent, Pierre.
French: habitational name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, denoting someone from any of several places called Bar (see Bar ).

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Duc
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Romanian: nickname from cuc ‘cuckoo’.
Amerindian (Guatemala; also Cúc): Mayan name, from a Castilianized form of kuk ‘squirrel’; compare Kuk .

variants.name: Kuk
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Song, Hyung, Jae, Kam, Keum, Man, Min Soo, Myung Ho, Seung, Soon, Sun, Sung.  German Siegfried, Horst, Otto.
North German: perhaps an occupational name for a pastry cook (compare   Kok   1).
Slovenian: probably a topographic name from dialect kuk ‘hip’, by extension ‘hill, hillock’ (compare Kok 2). It may also be a nickname from the homonym kuk ‘insect, little worm’.

nameMeanings.name: Marguerite
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of Margaret , also used in the English-speaking world, where its use has been reinforced by the fact that the name was adopted in the 19th century for a garden flower, a large cultivated variety of daisy. Margaret was earlier used in English as a dialect word denoting the ox-eye daisy, and the French equivalent was borrowed into English just in time to catch the vogue for deriving girls' names from vocabulary words denoting flowers. See also Daisy .

en.lifeSummary: Marguerite de Bar le Duc was born in Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lorraine, France as the daughter of Thiébaut Ier de Bar and Ermesende de Luxembourg I. She married Henri de Dampierre von Salm III in 1221. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died after 1259, and was buried in Arlon, Luxembourg, Belgium.

fr.lifeSummary: Marguerite de Bar le Duc est née à Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lorraine, France, fille de Thiébaut Ier de Bar et Ermesende de Luxembourg I. Elle a épousé Henri de Dampierre von Salm III en 1221. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée après 1259, et a été enterrée à Arlon, Luxembourg, Belgique.

de.lifeSummary: Marguerite de Bar le Duc wurde in Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lothringen, Frankreich, als Tochter von Thiébaut Ier de Bar und Ermesende de Luxembourg I geboren. Sie hat 1221 Henri de Dampierre von Salm III geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb nach 1259 und wurde in Arlon, Luxemburg, Belgien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Marguerite de Bar le Duc è nata il nel Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lorraine, Francia come figlia di Thiébaut Ier de Bar e Ermesende de Luxembourg I. Lei sposato Henri de Dampierre von Salm III nel 1221. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì dopo il 1259, e fu sepolta a Arlon, Lussemburgo, Belgio.

ja.lifeSummary: Marguerite de Bar le Ducはフランスロレィヌメウゼバー-レ-ドウックでThiébaut Ier de BarとErmesende de Luxembourg Iの娘として生まれた。1221年にHenri de Dampierre von Salm IIIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1259年以後に死去し，ベルギールクセンボウルグアルロンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Marguerite de Bar le Duc(은)는 프랑스 로레이인 우스 바-레-드크에서 Thiébaut Ier de Bar(와)과 Ermesende de Luxembourg I의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1221년 Henri de Dampierre von Salm III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1259년 이후 사망했으며 벨기에 루세보우르그 알런에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Marguerite de Bar le Duc nasceu em Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lorraine, França filha de Thiébaut Ier de Bar e Ermesende de Luxembourg I. Ela casou-se com Henri de Dampierre von Salm III em 1221. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu após 1259, e foi sepultada em Arlon, Luxemburgo, Bélgica.

ru.lifeSummary: Marguerite de Bar le Duc родилась в местности бар-ле-дук, меус, лорреаин, Франция. Её родителей звали Thiébaut Ier de Bar и Ermesende de Luxembourg I. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henri de Dampierre von Salm III в 1221 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла после 1259 г. и была похоронена в местности арлон, луксембург, Бельгия.

es.lifeSummary: Marguerite de Bar le Duc nació en Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lorraine, Francia como hija de Thiébaut Ier de Bar y Ermesende de Luxembourg I. Se casó con Henri de Dampierre von Salm III en 1221. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió después de 1259, y fue sepultada en Arlon, Luxemburgo, Bélgica.
